etymology.description: 美(美) (měi, "beautiful; America") + 元(元) (yuán, "currency unit; origin") creates the term for 'US dollar', emphasizing America as both the location associated with the currency and 'beautiful', which historically ties to the perception of America.
etymology.interpretation: 美(美) (měi, "beautiful; America") is used here as a phonetic and semantic element representing America, stemming from usage where 美洲 (měizhōu, "America") reinforces this link. 元(元) (yuán, "currency unit; origin") refers to money; the core or fundamental unit. The combination thus signifies the primary monetary unit of the United States.

examples.chinese: 我有一百美元(我有一百美元)。
examples.english: I have one hundred US dollars.

examples.chinese: 美元对人民币的汇率(美元對人民幣的匯率)，最近有所改变(最近有所改變)。
examples.pinyin: Měiyuán duì rénmínbì de huìlǜ, zuìjìn yǒu suǒ gǎibiàn.
examples.english: The exchange rate of the US dollar to RMB has changed recently.

examples.chinese: 许多人用美元储蓄(許多人用美元儲蓄)，因为它的稳定性(因為它的穩定性)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō rén yòng měiyuán chǔxù, yīnwèi tā de wěndìngxìng.
examples.english: Many people save in US dollars because of its stability.
